title: Extraction and Measurement of Polyphosphate and Inorganic Phosphorus from Microalgae Samples
author: Yingyu Hu
materials: Mix the reagents into Falcon tube: Reagent Parts in volume 1 MilliQ 2 2 6N sulphuric acid 1 3 2.5% ammonium molybdate 1 4 10% ascorbic acid 1
Flash freeze filters and stored at -80 °C
Read fluorescence: excitation at 410 nm and emission at 550 nm Varioskan LUX Multimode Microplate Reader (Thermo Fisher, VL0L00D0)
In Set S, add 36 µL Proteinase
In Set B, add 30 µL Proteinase

2.5 % ammonium molybdate reagent: Weigh 0.25 g ammonium molybdate in a Falcon tube and top to 10 g with MilliQ water. Cap and shake until totally dissolved. Ammonium molybdate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #09878-100G (CAS: 12054-85-2)

10 % ascorbic acid reagent: Weigh 1 g ascorbic acid in a Falcon tube and top to 10 g with MilliQ water; Cap and shake until all dissolved. Ascorbic acid Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A5960-100G (CAS: 50-81-7)

steps.title: Filter microalgae in liquid media onto...
steps.instruction: Filter microalgae in liquid media onto GF/F or PC filters, using gentle vacuum pressure (130 mmHg). Filter forceps (blunt end, stainless steel, Millipore, XX6200006P)

steps.title: Rinse sample with filtered saline (no nutrients)
steps.instruction: Rinse sample with filtered saline (no nutrients)

steps.title: Place sample filters in cryogenic vials
steps.instruction: Place sample filters in cryogenic vials

steps.title: Filter same volume of the blank...
steps.instruction: Filter same volume of the blank media (without cells) through GF/F or PC filter as blank.

steps.title: Freeze dry before measurement
steps.instruction: Freeze dry before measurement. FreeZone® 2.5 L Benchtop Freeze Dryers (Labconco®, 700202000)

steps.title: Tris buffer 20 mM pH 7.0 Budget
steps.instruction: Tris buffer 20 mM pH 7.0 Budget: About 250 mL per four samples

steps.title: In a 1 L volumetric flask,...
steps.instruction: In a 1 L volumetric flask, top 20 mL 1 M pH 7.0 Tris buffer to 1 L with MilliQ

steps.title: Store at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Store at Room temperature

steps.title: Weigh one glass pellet of polyP...
steps.instruction: Weigh one glass pellet of polyP (45) and write down the weight. Microbalance (Cubis series, Sartorius, MSE6.6S-000-DM)

steps.title: Transfer the pellet into a 100 mL graduated cylinder
steps.instruction: Transfer the pellet into a 100 mL graduated cylinder.

steps.title: Dilute to 100 mL with Tris 20 mM pH 7
steps.instruction: Dilute to 100 mL with Tris 20 mM pH 7.0

steps.title: Aliquot primary stock into 10~50 uL...
steps.instruction: Aliquot primary stock into 10~50 uL per microtube with Stepper and store at -20 °C

steps.title: PolyP secondary standard stock If the...
steps.instruction: PolyP secondary standard stock If the pellet is far more than 10 mg, dilute primary to secondary to bring down the concentration before preparing working standard

steps.title: Add 25 mL MilliQ directly into...
steps.instruction: Add 25 mL MilliQ directly into the original package of Proteinase K, vortex to mix

steps.title: Aliquot 700 uL to microtubes and...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 700 uL to microtubes and keep frozen at -20 °C

steps.title: DAPI primary stock 14
steps.instruction: DAPI primary stock 14.3 mM Add 2 mL MilliQ directly into the original package and keep frozen at -20 °C

steps.title: Prepare boiling bath
steps.instruction: Prepare boiling bath. VWR® Advanced Hot Plates (VWR, 97042-658) Hollow Polypropylene (PP) Ball Bath Covers, 20 mm (Cole-Parmer, UZ-06821-04) Tube rack (Simport MultiRack™, CA48648-606)

steps.title: Transfer sample into glass centrifuge tube...
steps.instruction: Transfer sample into glass centrifuge tube Disposable Glass Screw-Cap Centrifuge Tubes (10 mL, Corning®, 99502-10)

steps.title: Add 4 mL Tris buffer 20 mM pH 7
steps.instruction: Add 4 mL Tris buffer 20 mM pH 7.0 , vortex and then sonicate. Specific Pipette Tips 5mL (Thermo Scientific™ Finntip™, 21-377-304)

steps.title: Keep in boiling bath
steps.instruction: Keep in boiling bath. Make sure the tube rack is in the middle of the boiling bath and covered with PP balls. Tris solution in the tube should be boiling during the 5 minutes' incubation.

steps.title: Sonicate
steps.instruction: Sonicate

steps.title: Vortex and then transfer extract to...
steps.instruction: Vortex and then transfer extract to 15 mL Falcon tube, according to the extract number. Do not push filter to the bottom. Use Pasteur pipet, gently lift the filter upwards, and then transfer as much extract as possible. Gently press the extract out of the filter. Disposable Soda-Lime Glass Pasteur Pipets (5 3/4", Fisherbrand, 13-678-6A)

steps.title: Repeat Step 17 to Step 20...
steps.instruction: Repeat Step 17 to Step 20 until complete 15 times' extraction in total.

steps.title: Use forward pipetting, load black microtitre...
steps.instruction: Use forward pipetting, load black microtitre plate with 200 µL supernatant from the extract (one well for one extract, no need to load replicates). Tris buffer 20 mM pH 7.0 is used as blank. 96-Well Black Microplates (Polystyrene, Greiner Bio-One, 655076)

steps.title: Prepare DAPI working solution 100 uM Dilute 12
steps.instruction: Prepare DAPI working solution 100 uM Dilute 12.6 µL of 14.3 mM DAPI stock with 1800 µL MilliQ in a foil wrapped microtube and vortex.

steps.title: In the dimmed room with only...
steps.instruction: In the dimmed room with only red light bulb on add 24 µL 100 uM DAPI to each sample in the plate.

steps.title: Adhere black film on the top...
steps.instruction: Adhere black film on the top of a microplate lid and cover the plate with this lid. Black Vinyl Films for Fluorescence and Photoprotection (VWR, 89087-692)

steps.title: Shake at room temperature for 00:07:00
steps.instruction: Shake at room temperature for 00:07:00

steps.title: Plot fluorescence intensity versus number of extraction
steps.instruction: Plot fluorescence intensity versus number of extraction. The number of extract (N) is the stationary point where the fluorescence of stained extract stops decreasing or the derivative of the fluorescence after that point is close to zero. If RFU(15)-RFU(Tris)>1 , proceed to extract five additional times. And then measure the stained extract following the previous steps.

steps.title: Well mix 1~N extract, transfer 12...
steps.instruction: Well mix 1~N extract, transfer 12 mL into 15 mL falcon tube, centrifuge 3200 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes

steps.title: In Set S, add 18 µL...
steps.instruction: In Set S, add 18 µL RNase and 18 µL DNase RNase tends to leave residue in the tip. However one package has only 1 mL RNase, it will be a waste to use reverse pipetting. After dispensing RNase into the vial, use the same tip to draw the solution and gently dispense it back into the solution for about three time, so that there is no residue remaining in the tip. Replace a new tip for the next vial. Require ~400 uL RNase and ~400 uL DNase.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C , shake...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C , shake continuously SHAKING INCUBATOR (71L, Corning® LSE™, 6753) Start the timer when temperature reaches 37 °C

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C , shake continuously
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C , shake continuously. Start the timer when temperature reaches 37 °C

steps.title: Prepare DAPI working solution 100 uM Dilute 12
steps.instruction: Prepare DAPI working solution 100 uM Dilute 12.6 µL of 14.3 mM DAPI stock with 1800 µL MilliQ in a foil wrapped microtube and vortex.

steps.title: Prepare PolyP working standard [PO3]~ 7
steps.instruction: Prepare PolyP working standard [PO3]~ 7.6 uM Based on the actual concentration of PolyP (45) primary or secondary standard stock, dilute a certain volume of stock with Tris buffer 20 mM pH 7.0 For a final concentration 7.6 uM Total volume = 160 X N (ul), where N = sample number FW(45Na2O.55P2O5)=10600 Mol of PO3 per mol of PolyP (45) = 110

steps.title: Transfer 840 µL of enzyme treated...
steps.instruction: Transfer 840 µL of enzyme treated extract (1~N) into 2 mL tubes (Set A). Forward pipetting, aspire and dispense for three times to mix.

steps.title: Add 160 µL 7
steps.instruction: Add 160 µL 7.6 uM polyP working standard to 840 µL of enzyme treated extract, vortex.

steps.title: Load 200 µL blanks (B
steps.instruction: Load 200 µL blanks (B: N+1), samples (S: 1~N) and amended samples (A: Amended 1~N) to the microplate. Organize samples as shown in the following scheme: Reverse pipetting

steps.title: In a dimmed room with only...
steps.instruction: In a dimmed room with only red bulb on, add 24 µL DAPI working solution 100 uM to each sample in the microplate except for those labelled with (UN) . Forward pipetting

steps.title: Adhere black film on the top...
steps.instruction: Adhere black film on the top of a microplate lid and cover the plate with this lid.

steps.title: Shake at room temperature for 00:07:00
steps.instruction: Shake at room temperature for 00:07:00

steps.title: Shake duration
steps.instruction: Shake duration: 1 min Shaking type: continuous Shaking speed and force: 600 rpm/High Fluorescence: excitation at 410 nm and emission at 550 nm Measurement time: 300 ms Excitation bandwidth: 5 nm

steps.title: Centrifuge the extract 3200 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the extract 3200 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes

steps.title: Centrifuge extract "N+1" 3200 rpm, Room...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge extract "N+1" 3200 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes Blank is duplicated into B1a and B1b; B2a and B2b... etc.

steps.title: Transfer 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 1.8 mL supernatant to a 2 mL tube (Set S). Sample is triplicated into S1a, S1b and S1c; S2a, S2b, S2c...etc.

steps.title: Transfer 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 1.5 mL supernatant into a 2 mL tube (Set B).

steps.title: In Set B, add 15 µL...
steps.instruction: In Set B, add 15 µL RNase and 15 µL DNase

steps.title: Combine Extraction 1 to Extraction N into a falcon tube
steps.instruction: Combine Extraction 1 to Extraction N into a falcon tube. Try to transfer all solution including debris from each tube. If the total volume is over 50 mL, use a beaker instead. Sample code N V(Tris) per extract (mL) 1 2 3 4

steps.title: Label centrifuge tube for different samples,...
steps.instruction: Label centrifuge tube for different samples, place one Pasteur pipet into the tube for transferring extract from the same sample

steps.title: Label 15 mL Falcon tube from...
steps.instruction: Label 15 mL Falcon tube from 1 to 15 for each one sample.

steps.title: Prepare 37 °C incubator/shaker
steps.instruction: Prepare 37 °C incubator/shaker.

steps.title: Transfer 500 µL supernatant to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer 500 µL supernatant to a 2 mL microtube for Inorganic P measurement. Label each tube as 1-1, 1-2, ..., 1-N 2-1, 2-2, ..., 2-N 3-1, 3-2, ..., 3-N 4-1, 4-2, ..., 4-N

steps.title: KH 2 PO 4 primary standard...
steps.instruction: KH 2 PO 4 primary standard stock solution (≈ 1 mM) Potassium dihydrogen orthophosphate ACP Chemicals Catalog #P-4550 (CAS: 7778-77-0)

steps.title: Transfer about 1 g KH 2...
steps.instruction: Transfer about 1 g KH 2 PO 4 into a beaker, cover the beaker with foil

steps.title: Place the beaker into an oven,...
steps.instruction: Place the beaker into an oven, dry KH 2 PO 4 at 110 °C for at least 02:00:00

steps.title: Move KH 2 PO 4 into...
steps.instruction: Move KH 2 PO 4 into a vacuum desiccator, allow KH 2 PO 4 to cool to room temperature

steps.title: Dissolve around 0
steps.instruction: Dissolve around 0.136 g dried KH 2 PO 4 in 1 L milliQ water. Use 1 L volumetric flask Take notes of the actual weight of KH 2 PO 4 for final concentration of standard stock solution

steps.title: Transfer standard stock solution into a...
steps.instruction: Transfer standard stock solution into a 1 L bottle and store in the fridge. This stock solution lasts quite a long time, unless there is evidence for growth of algae or other extraneous biotic material.

steps.title: Standard working solution KH2PO4 Primary (uL)...
steps.instruction: Standard working solution KH2PO4 Primary (uL) MilliQ (uL) 1 S1 0 1000 2 S2 5 995 3 S3 10 990 4 S4 20 980 5 S5 50 950 6 S6 100 900 7 S7 150 850 8 S8 200 800

steps.title: Transfer 500 uL of each standard...
steps.instruction: Transfer 500 uL of each standard working solution to 2 mL microtube.

steps.title: Remove extract from the fridge and...
steps.instruction: Remove extract from the fridge and warm up to room temperature.

steps.title: 6 N (3 M) sulfuric acid reagent
steps.instruction: 6 N (3 M) sulfuric acid reagent: Carefully add 1 part 18 M concentrated sulfuric acid into 5 part MilliQ water Save the pipet tip for final reagent preparation.

steps.title: Calculate the volume of molybdate-ascorbic reagent
steps.instruction: Calculate the volume of molybdate-ascorbic reagent: Total volume of reagent_mL = (0.5 mL) X (#standard working solution + #samples + #blanks)

steps.title: Add 500 µL reagent to each...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL reagent to each standard, sample and blank, starting from blanks, including blank for standards and blank for samples. Finntip Stepper Tips (5 mL, Thermo Scientific, 9404200) Before dispensing the reagent, wipe or dab the liquid drop on the outside of the tip, avoid wiping the open tip.

steps.title: Vortex each tube
steps.instruction: Vortex each tube

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 03:00:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 03:00:00 while shaking at 200 rpm

steps.title: Load microplate with 250 uL reactant from each tube, duplicate
steps.instruction: Load microplate with 250 uL reactant from each tube, duplicate. Example of loading the microplate

steps.title: Read plate in microplate reader A...
steps.instruction: Read plate in microplate reader A B 1 Shake duration 00:00:05 2 Shaking type Continuous 3 Shaking force High 4 Shaking speed [rpm] 600 5 Wavelength [nm] 820 6 Use transmittance No 7 Pathlength correction No 8 Measurement Time [ms] 100

steps.title: Subtract the average absorbance at 820...
steps.instruction: Subtract the average absorbance at 820 nm of the blank standard replicates from the absorbance at 820 nm of all other standard working solutions.

steps.title: Subtract the average absorbance at 820...
steps.instruction: Subtract the average absorbance at 820 nm of the blank sample (i.e. blank filter) replicates from the absorbance at 820 nm of all other individual samples.

steps.title: Prepare a standard curve by plotting...
steps.instruction: Prepare a standard curve by plotting the average blank-corrected 820 nm absorbance for each standard working solution versus its concentration in uM.

steps.title: Use the standard curve to determine...
steps.instruction: Use the standard curve to determine the orthophosphate concentration of each unknown sample by using its blank-corrected 820 nm absorbance.

22.5% polyP can be measured by molybdate assay, possibly due to the partial hydrolysis in acid condition Where, Pi umol is the concentration of inorganic P, Pj uM is inorganic P from each extract, Vj is the volume of Tris, n is the total extraction times, polyP umol is the polyP concentration in the sample.
